## Mode: <light-spec / feature-spec / full-spec>

## Summary
<2-3 sentence overall assessment of spec completeness and implementation readiness>

---

## Findings

### CRITICAL (must fix before proceeding)
<!-- Each item blocks CONFIRM. If this section is empty, write "None." -->
- [ ] <finding>

### WARNING (should address, but not blocking)
<!-- Recommended fixes. Can proceed with CONFIRM if owner acknowledges these. -->
- [ ] <finding>

### SUGGESTION (nice to have)
<!-- Low-priority improvements. Can safely ignore. -->
- [ ] <finding>

---

## Checklist

### light-spec
- [ ] Business justification is clear
- [ ] Scope and non-scope are defined
- [ ] Impacted files are listed
- [ ] Risks are identified
- [ ] Backward compatibility is confirmed

### feature-spec (in addition to above)
- [ ] Architecture decision is sound
- [ ] Test plan covers key paths
- [ ] Acceptance criteria are testable
- [ ] Performance impact is acceptable
- [ ] Security surface is reviewed

### full-spec (in addition to above)
- [ ] Data model is consistent with design
- [ ] API contracts are complete
- [ ] Cross-team dependencies are signed off
- [ ] Rollout plan has rollback procedure
- [ ] Monitoring plan is defined

---

## Decision: [CONFIRM / REVISE / REJECT]

<!-- Set by human after reviewing findings above. -->

**If CONFIRM**:
- light-spec → run `/aidlc-build <change-id>`
- feature-spec / full-spec → run `/aidlc-plan <change-id>`

**If REVISE**: <specify which findings to address and which command to re-run>

**If REJECT**: <reason> — archive the change as rejected.
